meta.notes: Editable — add your own items following docs/question-format.md

questions.prompt: Which simple figure is hidden inside the complex diagram?
questions.explanation: A right triangle is embedded within the overlapping lines of the diagram.
questions.hint: Trace straight edges and look for a three-sided shape.

questions.prompt: Which option shows the correct mirror image of the figure on the left (mirror placed on the right side)?
questions.explanation: In a vertical mirror reflection, left and right are swapped; Option B matches the reversal.
questions.hint: Imagine flipping the figure horizontally.

questions.prompt: Which pair of symbols contains identical information (same shape and orientation)?
questions.explanation: Pair 3 shows two identical arrows pointing in the same direction with the same size.
questions.hint: Compare both shape and direction, not just shape alone.

questions.prompt: Which letter shape can be found hidden in the figure?
questions.explanation: The horizontal and vertical bars form a hidden letter T within the pattern.
questions.hint: Look for perpendicular line segments.

questions.prompt: Which figure is the mirror image of the arrow pointing northeast?
questions.explanation: Reflecting northeast across a vertical axis gives northwest.
questions.hint: Swap east and west directions.

questions.prompt: How many pairs in the set are exactly identical?
questions.explanation: Three pairs are exactly identical: the blue circles, green triangles, and purple triangles.
questions.hint: Check orientation carefully — rotated shapes are not identical.

questions.prompt: Which option is the mirror image of the given shape?
questions.explanation: The reflected shape is the one with the left-right reversal while preserving orientation of the internal marks.
questions.hint: Mirror images reverse left and right.

questions.prompt: How many shapes in this grid are exactly the same orientation and size?
questions.explanation: Four shapes match exactly in size and orientation while others are rotated or mirrored.
questions.hint: Ignore rotated and mirrored versions.

questions.prompt: Which simple shape is hidden within the overlapping design?
questions.explanation: The overlapping design contains a triangle formed by three intersecting lines and angles.
questions.hint: Look for the simplest closed shape among the overlapping segments.

questions.prompt: A right-facing arrow is reflected across a vertical mirror. Which direction does the image point?
questions.explanation: A vertical mirror swaps left and right, so a right-facing arrow becomes left-facing.
questions.hint: Mirror reflection reverses horizontal direction.

questions.prompt: How many triangles in the set have the same orientation?
questions.explanation: Only two triangles share the same size and orientation while the others are rotated differently.
questions.hint: Count orientation as well as shape.

questions.prompt: Which letter is hidden in the overlapping shapes?
questions.explanation: The overlapping lines form the shape of the letter E when viewed as a whole.
questions.hint: Look for three horizontal lines connected by one vertical line.

questions.prompt: If a shape faces upward and is reflected across a horizontal mirror, which direction does the image face?
questions.explanation: A horizontal mirror reflection flips the image vertically, so the upward-facing shape appears downward-facing.
questions.hint: Mirror reflections flip the axis opposite the mirror line.

questions.prompt: How many items in the grid are identical in both shape and orientation?
questions.explanation: Three items share the same shape and orientation while the others are rotated or mirrored.
questions.hint: Ignore rotated duplicates and focus on exact matches.

questions.prompt: Which simple shape is hidden in the abstract pattern?
questions.explanation: The overlapping lines form a triangular shape that is easiest to see when following the three outer edges.
questions.hint: Count the sides of the hidden shape.

questions.prompt: A leftward arrow is reflected across a vertical mirror. Which direction does the image point?
questions.explanation: A vertical mirror reflection swaps left and right, so a leftward arrow becomes rightward.
questions.hint: Mirror reflections reverse the horizontal direction.

questions.prompt: Which pair of shapes is exactly identical in orientation and size?
questions.explanation: Pair 4 has two shapes that match exactly in both orientation and size while the others differ in one property.
questions.hint: Check both direction and scale before choosing.

questions.prompt: Which letter shape is hidden in the overlapping elements?
questions.explanation: The overlapping vertical and diagonal lines form the letter A.
questions.hint: Look for a pointed top and a crossbar.

questions.prompt: If a T-shape is reflected across a vertical mirror, which figure matches its image?
questions.explanation: The mirror image reverses the horizontal orientation of the T while preserving vertical symmetry.
questions.hint: Flip the shape as if it were reflected in glass.

questions.prompt: How many circles in the set are identical in size and position?
questions.explanation: Three circles share the same size and placement while one is slightly shifted or resized.
questions.hint: Compare exact placement as well as size.

questions.prompt: Which simple shape is formed by the overlapping diagonal lines?
questions.explanation: The diagonal lines intersect to form a triangular outline inside the pattern.
questions.hint: Trace the three sides that make a closed shape.

questions.prompt: A shape points diagonally up-right and is reflected in a vertical mirror. Which direction does the image point?
questions.explanation: A vertical mirror flips right to left, so up-right becomes up-left.
questions.hint: Only the horizontal component changes direction.

questions.prompt: Which pair of shapes is entirely identical?
questions.explanation: Pair 1 shows the same shape with the same orientation and size, while the other pairs differ in one aspect.
questions.hint: Exact replication means no rotations or size changes.

questions.prompt: Which simple shape appears hidden inside the arranged polygons?
questions.explanation: The shape of a triangle can be traced among the overlapping polygon edges.
questions.hint: Find the three connecting line segments.

questions.prompt: Which figure is the correct mirror image of the original shape across a horizontal axis?
questions.explanation: The correct mirror image flips the vertical orientation while keeping the horizontal shape the same.
questions.hint: Flip the image across a horizontal line.

questions.prompt: How many shapes are identical to the target shape in the set?
questions.explanation: Only one shape in the set exactly matches both the orientation and size of the target.
questions.hint: Compare each shape carefully to the reference form.

questions.prompt: Which simple geometric shape is hidden within the tessellated pattern?
questions.explanation: A diamond shape is formed by the overlapping diagonals and is the hidden figure in the pattern.
questions.hint: Look for a four-sided shape with equal opposite angles.

questions.prompt: A vertical arrow is reflected across a horizontal mirror. Which option is the image?
questions.explanation: A horizontal mirror flips the vertical direction, so an upward arrow becomes downward.
questions.hint: The reflection changes the component perpendicular to the mirror line.

questions.prompt: Which option contains two shapes that are identical in every way?
questions.explanation: Option A shows two shapes that match exactly in size, orientation, and styling.
questions.hint: Inspect both shape and orientation for an exact match.

questions.prompt: Which simple form is hidden inside the busy composition?
questions.explanation: The overlapping edges form a triangle that is the hidden shape in the composition.
questions.hint: Search for a three-sided figure inside the pattern.
